By Anna Edwards. PUBLISHED: 04:51 EST, 2 August 2012 | UPDATED: 07:14 EST, 2 August 2012.
A bridegroom died in an horrific motorcycle crash two days after his 'fairytale' wedding. Newlywed Phillip John Cleary, 34, died after his motorbike was hit by two cars on a country road two days after marrying his fiancee Gemma. Police believe he lost control of his motorbike and collided with one car before hitting another. Mr Cleary, of Howden, near Hull, Yorkshire, was pronounced dead at the crash scene on the A164, between Howden and Middleton on the Wolds, on Monday evening.
Tragedy: Phillip Cleary died in a motorcycle accident, days after marrying his beautiful partner Gemma.
Hundreds have joined a Facebook page, 'In Memory of Phill Cleary,' paying tribute to him.
His sister Lisa Cleary wrote: 'My big brother. You have grown up to be such a kind-hearted, funny and generous man with such a great spirit. 'You were taken way too young and at such a special time in your life. 'You will be in my heart and in my soul forever. We will all miss you so much.' She later posted: 'Phill, it's very apparent that you touched so many people's lives and will leave a huge hole in all our hearts. 'Everyone is saying you are a legend and I have to agree. 'You will never get old and grey, you will be remembered as the 34-year-old man, who achieved so much, met the love of his life and got to marry her in front of many smiling faces. 'I am so proud to be able to call you my brother and you will live on in our hearts forever.'
Mr Cleary was originally from east Hull and was the managing partner of The Citadel Partnership, a health and safety consultancy for the construction industry. He is also believed to have worked as a doorman at pubs and clubs in Hull city centre.
'Lost control': The scene of the fatal collision on the A614 outside Middlethorpe Farm where Mr Cleary passed away.
Scene of the accident: Family and friends have taken to Facebook to pay their respects to the popular newlywed.
His mum Sue Cleary, of east Hull, simply wrote: 'My darling son I love and miss you so much.'
Friend Richard Giles posted: 'I will never forget your trembling bottom lip when you saw Gemma walking down the aisle. 'In my drunken state, I was thinking of sneaking off to bed on Saturday without saying goodbye, if I did I wouldn't have got to say how happy I am for you and proud I was to be part of your big day.'
Ruth Colarieti, of San Luca restaurant in Hessle, said Mr and Mrs Cleary were looking forward to their wedding during their visit to the restaurant last week. She wrote: 'So shocked and saddened to hear of this devastating news. 'Phil and Gemma were in the restaurant only last week telling us how excited they both were for their wedding on Saturday. Such a lovely couple with the world at their feet.'
A Humberside Police spokesman said Mr Cleary was riding a blue Honda motorcycle on the A614 from Howden towards Middleton on the Wolds when it is believed he lost control. He then collided with a silver Toyota Rav traveling in the opposite direction, followed by a blue Ford Focus, also traveling in the opposite direction. Mr Cleary died at the scene. Occupants of the other vehicles were not injured.

**Campus Security Policy Debate: Concealed Carry on University Grounds**

A growing movement advocates for expanded concealed carry rights on college campuses, with proponents arguing that licensed individuals should have the ability to protect themselves in emergency situations. Michael Flitcraft, a University of Cincinnati student and organizer for Students for Concealed Carry on Campus, represents this perspective. The organization, formed following the Virginia Tech incident that resulted in 32 fatalities, now has over 25,000 members nationwide.

Flitcraft, who holds a valid concealed carry license in Ohio, highlights what he views as an inconsistency in current policy. While legally permitted to carry his weapon off-campus, doing so on university property would constitute a felony. This regulatory gap forms the basis of his advocacy efforts.

The campus security debate has gained momentum following high-profile incidents at Virginia Tech and Northern Illinois University. Currently, lawmakers in nine states are reviewing legislation to permit concealed carry on campus, while others have rejected such measures. Utah remains the only state allowing weapons at all public universities, with Colorado permitting carry at most institutions except the Boulder campus. Virginia's Blue Ridge Community College also allows properly licensed individuals to carry concealed weapons.

**Law Enforcement Perspective**

University of Cincinnati Police Chief Gene Ferrara presents the opposing viewpoint, emphasizing prevention over armed response. With over twelve years of municipal law enforcement experience, Ferrara advocates for focusing resources on preventing incidents rather than responding to them. He raises operational concerns about distinguishing between perpetrators and licensed civilians during active shooter situations, noting the risk of misidentification in high-stress scenarios.

Ferrara's department prioritizes educational outreach and anonymous reporting systems for suspicious behavior, citing research indicating that warning signs typically precede violent incidents.

**Student and Faculty Response**

Campus sentiment appears divided on the issue. Many students express concern about the proposal, while security officials note that university environments statistically remain among the safest locations for young adults. Since the 1966 University of Texas incident, approximately twelve shooting events have occurred at higher education institutions nationwide.

At Weber State University in Utah, where concealed carry is permitted, faculty member Ron Holt views firearms as protective insurance, acknowledging that such measures would rarely be necessary.

**Current Advocacy Efforts**

Students for Concealed Carry on Campus continues expanding its reach through social media platforms and organized demonstrations. The group specifically advocates for students aged 21 and older with proper licensing, rather than universal campus carry. Planned protests will feature supporters wearing empty holsters to symbolize their position.

The organization frames the debate as a risk management question: whether controlled defensive capability outweighs potential risks in emergency situations.

# Toure Brothers Play On Despite Family Tragedy

Yaya and Kolo Toure both started for Ivory Coast in their crucial Group C showdown with Greece just days after the death of their brother. Ibrahim Toure, a 28-year-old player with the Lebanon-based Al Safa, died in Manchester on Thursday. He had been suffering from cancer. 

Reports had claimed the brothers would leave Brazil to be with their family, but the duo have vowed to play on in a bid to help their country reach the last 16.

## Decision to Stay and Fight

Playing on: Yaya Toure decided to stay in Brazil despite the death of his brother.

Staying strong: Kolo Toure lined up for Ivory Coast against Greece.

VIDEO City didn't let me visit my dying brother - Toure

## Tribute to Ibrahim

Ivory Coast's player wore black armbands in tribute to Ibrahim.

## Yaya's Criticism of Manchester City

Yaya Toure has suggested that Manchester City did not offer him sufficient support during a period which he describes as 'the hardest in my life.'

He told France Football: 'At the end of the season, I wanted to stay for four or five days with my brother before I flew to prepare for the World Cup with Ivory Coast, except that City did not want to grant me a few days.

Tribute: The Ivory Coast team wore black armbands as a mark of respect.

'I went to celebrate the title championship in Abu Dhabi while my brother was lying in his sickbed. By fortune, Kolo was at his bedside. After that I blame myself for not insisting and for not making them respect me.

'However, club officials knew very well that I was suffering for a few months as the health of my brother declined. This is the reason I had several injuries at the end of the season, because my head had taken control of my body. These last four months have probably been the hardest in my life.

## Fan Support

Support: Fans held up a sign for the Toure brothers at the game.

The main points of the story, explained with real examples, are:

1.  **Satire of political absurdity and the "slippery slope" fallacy:** The story uses the extreme scenario of a cat becoming president to humorously critique the current political climate and the "slippery slope" arguments often used in societal debates. The narrator mocks pundits who warned of marrying toasters, only to ironically wonder if they were right when her cat is elected.
    *   **Real example:** Debates around topics like same-sex marriage or cannabis legalization often featured "slippery slope" arguments, with opponents claiming they would lead to absurd or catastrophic outcomes (e.g., marrying inanimate objects, widespread societal collapse). The story takes this to its literal, absurd conclusion.

2.  **The pervasive influence of internet and meme culture:** The ending, with the narrator dressing Sprinkles in a top hat for Reddit, suggests that online virality and meme culture can profoundly impact public perception and even seemingly impossible events. The cat's presidency is implicitly a product of internet fame.
    *   **Real example:** The rise of internet memes influencing political discourse (e.g., "dark Brandon" memes, Pepe the Frog), the collective power of online communities to push agendas (e.g., the GameStop stock phenomenon), or the way viral social media content can elevate unknown figures to widespread recognition, sometimes with real-world consequences.

3.  **The profound human-animal bond and anthropomorphism:** Sprinkles is portrayed as a crucial emotional support, a "pro cat" who understands human needs. The narrator's deep affection and reliance on her pet highlight the significant role animals play in many people's lives, often being treated as family members.
    *   **Real example:** The booming pet industry, the proliferation of emotional support animals, and how many people treat their pets as children, celebrating their birthdays, creating social media accounts for them, and confiding in them, underscoring their integral role in mental well-being and companionship.

4.  **Acceptance of eccentric family members:** The narrator's mom is depicted as eccentric, with her "schizophrenic prostitute thing" and bad jokes, yet she is clearly loved. This illustrates the complex and often humorous dynamics within families where quirks are accepted as part of identity.
    *   **Real example:** Many families have eccentric relatives—an aunt with unusual beliefs, a grandparent who repeats the same stories, or a parent with peculiar habits—whose idiosyncrasies are tolerated and even cherished out of love and familiarity, rather than being a source of serious conflict.

I loved Fido Friendly and have always looked forward to receiving it. I renewed my subscription in September 2011 and received one copy (I believe it was in October or November). That was it ... nada, nothing after that. I called Fido Friendly earlier this Spring, was told I'd get the next issue shortly, and never received anything. I made a second call two weeks later and got the same promise with no follow-through. I even checked with my postal carrier to make sure magazines weren't being misdelivered to neighbors. Finally after more than 7 or 8 months without receiving any magazines I cancelled my subscription and asked for a refund. This is particularly disappointing because I had subscribed for three years prior without any issues, and I recommended the magazine to several fellow dog owners who likely experienced the same problems. The lack of communication and failure to deliver a paid service is simply unacceptable in today's business environment. FWIW I'm giving 5 STARS to Magazine Customer Service ... Amazon.com Magazines ... Synapse Services which handled the subscription. They were on top of my cancellation request and within 24 hours I got a notice that a refund had been credited to my account!!! Their prompt response and professional handling of the situation shows how customer service should work - they clearly have systems in place to track subscriptions and process refunds efficiently, unlike the magazine publisher itself.

Post Office to Ban Emma Goldman’s “Mother Earth”

On this day, the U.S. Post Office issued a directive to Mother Earth, the influential anarchist magazine founded and edited by Emma Goldman, demanding that the publication “show cause” as to why it should not be banned from the mails. Mother Earth was known for its fierce opposition to U.S. involvement in World War I, as well as its advocacy for labor rights, birth control, free speech, and other radical causes. The federal government, invoking the recently enacted Espionage Act of June 15, 1917—which criminalized interference with the military draft and criticism of the war effort—had already targeted the publication and Goldman herself. Goldman and her close associate, Alexander Berkman, had been arrested in June 1917 for conspiring to “induce persons not to register” for the draft.

Citing anti-war articles and seditious content, the Post Office moved to revoke the magazine’s second-class mail privileges—a tool widely and effectively wielded during World War I as a form of censorship against dissenting publications. Loss of second-class status, which allowed for affordable distribution via the postal service, amounted to a practical ban, since it rendered continued publication financially and logistically impossible. After losing its mailing rights, Mother Earth was forced to suspend publication later in 1917.

The government’s crackdown on radicals intensified during the war years and in the postwar Red Scare. In December 1919, Emma Goldman—along with 248 other supposed “alien radicals”—was deported from the United States to Soviet Russia aboard the infamous “Red Ark” (the USAT Buford). Despite her hopes for a socialist revolution, Goldman soon became a harsh critic of Bolshevik repression in her subsequent writings and lectures.

Learn more at the Emma Goldman Archives: http://sunsite.berkeley.edu/goldman/
Read: Alice Wexler, Emma Goldman: An Intimate Life (1984)
Follow a timeline of Emma Goldman’s life and work: http://ucblibrary3.berkeley.edu/goldman/Curricula/timeline.hml
Learn more about Emma Goldman: http://www.pbs.org/wgbh/amex/goldman/sfeature/sf_motherearth.html

There's a man defecating on my desk. He's a three-inch-tall plastic Caganer figurine, a traditional Catalan nativity fixture, and just one of my interesting desk toys. But I've been missing out on the single greatest one of all: Buckyballs. This week, the interwebs started buzzing about them. The company announced they were discontinuing production of their hugely popular and addictive shape-changing magnetic beads. Think stress ball meets marbles meets Silly Putty... ...meets possible agonizing pain.

**Magnetic Buckyballs toys discontinued.**
The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has basically deemed these tiny magnetic balls of joy just slightly less dangerous than trying to administer a hernia test to an unwilling gorilla. In a July lawsuit against the makers of Buckyballs, the commission noted the magnets "contain a defect in the design, packaging, warnings and instructions which pose a substantial risk of injury to the public."

The core problem: kids like shiny things and sometimes swallow them. When those shiny things are superpowerful magnets, it's a fairly serious health concern. Many retailers voluntarily stopped selling Buckyballs at the safety commission's request, but the distributor, Maxfield & Oberton Holdings, continued to manufacture and sell the product.

Until Wednesday. Buckyballs then put out this statement: "Due to baseless and relentless legal badgering by a certain four letter government agency, it's time to bid a fond farewell to the world's most popular adult desk toys, Buckyballs and Buckycubes. That's right: We're sad to say that Balls & Cubes have a one-way ticket to the Land-of-Awesome-Stuff-You-Should-Have-Bought-When-You-Had-the-Chance."

Buckyball supporters were furious. Now I feel their pain. Because these things are amazing! I decided to step away from my work and scour the newsroom to see if anyone actually had Buckyballs to test them out. My colleague Michelle DID have Buckyballs! She wasn't in, so I politely stole them. Walking around the newsroom with this small mass of tiny round magnets was like coming to work with a puppy. Everyone wanted to hold it, and I watched seemingly mature adults delightfully morph into whimsical children, completely in awe of this strange, invisible force. For as the great American poets, Insane Clown Posse, once asked, "(Expletive) magnets -- how do they work?"

The irony is that it's real children who have brought Buckyballs to their end. Despite warning labels in five places on each box since 2010, and additional inserts specifically instructing adults to keep these far, far away from kids, Buckyballs are still being swallowed.

So, the debate rages online: Was it right for the government to go after Buckyballs, or has the company properly done its due diligence to warn and protect consumers? It's sort of a moot point since, at least for now, what they have in stock is all that's left. Though, I suppose you could always just politely steal them from Michelle's desk.

Tick. Tick… Ti—FUCK! In one fluid, desperate motion, Sebastian ripped the lamp from its socket and brought it crashing down on the clock beside him. Shards of plastic and glass scattered in an explosive punctuation. He had finally had enough—enough of time, enough of deadlines, enough of the relentless, man-made bullshit turning him into something feral. 

Ninety-three and a half hours had passed since he last slept. Now, Sebastian sat hollow-eyed before the monitor, its empty glow mirroring the vacancy inside him. Humanity slipped away, siphoned by exhaustion, replaced by the primal instincts of an animal driven to its edge. He glared at the screen, as if sheer force of will could intimidate the words into appearing. Those motherfucking words. If they refused to comply, he would rip them to shreds.

His eyebrows fused into a single furious line. A grotesque grin split his lips; his jaw locked until his teeth ground together, krrrrrrrrr, the sound raw and ragged in the quiet room. Hours earlier, Rockstar Energy had been his god, fueling what he believed was genius. Now, it was a demon gnawing his mind, leaving only a gritty residue behind. 

He clawed at his mouth, fingernails scraping against enamel. Horror replaced anger in his bloodshot eyes—his expression more animal than ever. He stared, dazed, at the flecks of white plaque under his nails, panic blossoming in his chest. 

His legs jolted into motion before his arms; he tripped over himself rising from the chair, stumbling toward the door and catching his weight on trembling fingers. The bathroom door ahead was closed—locked. 

"Open the fucking door!" He pounded once, twice, the sharp rap of desperation echoing off the apartment walls. Silence. Then, slowly, the door creaked open. 

He sidestepped past his roommate—this stranger—and snatched up his toothbrush. His eyes fell on the toothpaste next, pupils blown wide with frantic energy. Without a second thought, he squeezed a fifth of the tube straight into his mouth, foam choking whatever remained of his rational mind.

It is so easy to get caught up in the fast-paced world we live in. Eventually you start going through the motions without taking a moment to breathe. Meditation can be your personal daily sanctuary where you can get away from the day’s stresses (children, work, husband, financial woes) and become one with yourself.

I remember when I first started meditating, my days were a whirlwind of responsibilities. Between work deadlines, taking care of my kids, and maintaining a household, I barely had a moment to sit down, much less reflect. One particularly stressful afternoon, a friend suggested trying a short guided meditation. At first, I was skeptical—how could sitting in silence help me manage the chaos? But after just ten minutes of focusing on my breath, I noticed a significant shift in how I felt. My mind felt clearer, my body less tense. That initial experience became the foundation for a daily habit that now helps me navigate even the most challenging days.

Meditation has been a practice among many for thousands of years. Today it is used to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Once you’re able to clear your head of the random stream of thoughts that fill it, you can reach a state of deep relaxation and tranquility. Some believe it gives you a greater sense of awareness.

Take the story of Sam, a former colleague of mine, who struggled with anxiety and insomnia. After his doctor recommended mindfulness meditation, Sam committed to just five minutes each morning. Over several weeks, he not only found it easier to fall asleep, but reported feeling less overwhelmed by anxious thoughts during the day. His story is just one example of how meditation can bring meaningful change.

According to the Mayo Clinic, meditation’s mind-body connection also has many health benefits, both mental and physical.

When you meditate, you clear the information that overwhelms your mind and creates stress, which is good for your emotional well-being. It can also help you gain a new perspective on stressful situations, build skills to manage your stress, increase self-awareness, focus on the present and reduce negative emotions.

Meditation has also been shown to help alleviate physical ailments. It’s especially helpful if you have a medical condition that is worsened by stress. Some research shows that it can benefit you if you have:
- Anxiety disorders
- Binge eating
- Heart disease
- High blood pressure
- Sleep problems
- Substance abuse

Meditation isn’t a replacement for medical treatment, but in many cases can be used in addition to it. You should certainly speak to a doctor if you’re considering starting meditation and have a medical condition.

There are many different types of meditation as well as guides on how to start. Luckily, you don’t need anything to meditate; just a quiet space and yourself. As my own experience and those around me have shown, even a few minutes a day can make a profound difference in your quality of life.

Today, Monday, May 11, 2009, at 12:01 pm MDT, the Space Shuttle Atlantis soared skyward, embarking on its fourth—and what’s been designated its final—mission to renew the venerable, 20-year-old Hubble Space Telescope.

Since its deployment in 1990, Hubble has forever transformed our cosmic vision, unveiling marvels of the universe that ground-based observatories could scarcely hope to glimpse. In its earliest days, however, Hubble was met with disappointment and derision. Detractors called it a costly fiasco, focused on the revelations of its flawed optics. I recall, as a young planetarium educator, fielding questions and explaining Hubble's capabilities. Even in its imperfect state, it was already offering clearer insights into the universe than any rival telescope. Despite its rocky start, Hubble soon dazzled cynics and captivated the world. Its breathtaking images didn’t just fill astronomy textbooks; they graced the covers of Time, Newsweek, and mainstream outlets across the globe.

Over the years, Hubble has been serviced three times—the telescope was ingeniously engineered for astronauts to tinker, upgrade, and revitalize it. Each mission refined its vision. Engineers installed COSTAR to remedy its optical shortcomings, swapped in the improved Wide Field Planetary Camera 2, and later added the Advanced Camera for Surveys. Each innovation sharpened Hubble’s focus and elevated its reputation.

Now, with this final mission, astronauts are set to handle routine duties—replacing gyroscopes and batteries—but they’ll also tackle a suite of technological advancements. Critical sensors, circuit boards, and spectrographs await repair or replacement; a new insulation blanket will help shield Hubble for years to come. Most exciting of all, perhaps, is the installation of the Wide Field Planetary Camera 3, superseding WFPC2. This state-of-the-art camera will push the frontiers of our knowledge about dark matter and dark energy. As these upgrades come online, Hubble will once more deliver jaw-dropping views of galaxies and nebulas, answering some of our deepest questions—and surely inspiring new ones for the generations that follow. In eleven days, as Atlantis returns to Earth, Hubble will begin the final chapter of its extraordinary voyage.

Afterward, the telescope will undergo months of thorough testing, and we await the first stunning images from the “reborn” Hubble with mounting anticipation. Yet, as I reflect on this moment, it's clear we are witnessing the first steps toward the end of a legendary era—the closing chapters in the story of humanity’s first great space-based observatory.

For parents who think LSU's dining is great, they are not. You are better off eating at that crappy King Buffet by Mall of Louisiana than this piece of garbage. Sandy swiftly left her house, dodging fat raindrops that pounded against the regulated overhangs of each doorway. She had forgotten her umbrella in her haste not to be late — again. After a few more near misses, darting between the scant shelter of doorways, Sandy finally found herself panting at the doorway of an imposing, dull gray building. Her last chance to enter, when she was eight, had been missed by five minutes due to a shoelace malfunction. Due to that mistake, she had been forced to remain in her genetic nursery unit for an additional four years. Four years wasted with her genetic peers, when she could have been sorted by another category. The possibilities were endless, and such opportunities opened up only a few times in a lifetime. She straightened her collar and slacks, matching her pace to the crowd in front of her. To her left were posters of the options available this session. Imagine! There was once a time when people didn't know who they were because they didn't know what they could become! She brushed her hair from her eyes, tucking it back behind her ears. The tall once considered the short inferior, though their true disdain had been for the slow. The problem was not what one's neighbors were, but what one could *become*. Sandy peeked around the shoulders of the person ahead, watching as the crowd split into lines leading to their potential futures. Men, women, tall, short – every size and shape. A few mutants were interspersed among them, remnants from when the (*bless his passing*) Hero attacked civilization with his bombs. A few genetic Purities stood in their chosen lines, backs straight and eyes bright. Gifts from the sacrifice of the Princess (*bless her sacrifice*), who gave of herself so a new generation could be born with her ability to mutate only in a harmless manner. History taught that three nurseries of children came into being due to her sacrifice before its toll claimed her life. “Sandy. Explain yourself.” She was startled out of her historical musings at the sound of her name. It was her turn in front of her chosen placement board, and their stern faces shook her to her bones. If they determined she had chosen poorly, or without proper consideration, she would go to the end of the line to wait again. If she didn't get placed today, she would have to wait four *more* years before she could try again. “Ladies of the board,” Sandy started, her voice shaking and cracking. “I have chosen to leave my genetic male nursery and be placed into a women's living community.”

With aging highways and more money for public works projects, road construction sites are expected to grow. While this means safer roads for millions, road construction carries dangers. The interim during construction is risky for drivers and construction workers.

Accidents and Incidents by Numbers
Every year car crashes involving 700 fatalities and 37,000 injuries take place across the country, according to the American Road and Transportation Builders Association. Most accidents are caused by driver error—rear-ending incidents are significantly higher in construction zones, as the stop-and-go nature requires vigilant driving.

Other problems include speeding and aggressive driving. In 2005, speed was a factor in 27 percent of fatal crashes in construction zones. Aggressive driving—failing to yield, excessive lane changes—was another big cause, as these tactics in areas where lanes are reduced and traffic capacity is taxed lead to more incidents.

Strategies for Reducing Risk
The numbers of accidents are heading downward. In 2009, there were 667 fatalities from motor vehicle crashes in construction zones, a drop of 7.4 percent from 2008. These numbers have been falling every year over the past decade. Much of this success is due to efforts by governments and contractors to educate the public about construction site safety and make fines for dangerous driving in construction zones heftier.

These efforts include putting up construction barricades and signs that warn drivers of upcoming construction sites and using traffic barriers to make navigation through these zones easier. Many states have raised or doubled fines for speeding in construction zones to encourage drivers to slow down and be more aware. Some have added traffic cameras to further mitigate risk.

State and local governments and construction contractors are aware of safety dangers for everyone involved in road construction and work to reduce accidents and fatalities in these zones. Ongoing efforts include public education programs and effective use of highway resources to move drivers through sites as quickly and safely as possible.

His heart hammered a frantic rhythm against his ribs, a frantic drumbeat drowning out all else, as his breath hitched, ragged and shallow in his throat. His trembling fingers, slick with nervous sweat, clawed at the brittle pages, ripping through the old ledger, a frantic blur of faded ink and yellowed paper as he searched erratically. *"Where can it be? Where did she hide it?"* he thought, a desperate, raw whisper echoing in the suffocating silence of the small room. His worn, calloused hands, usually so steady, flew over the pages, leaving faint smudges, as his clear blue eyes, now wide and bloodshot, frantically scoured the familiar loops and angles of his own handwriting. He swallowed hard, a dry, painful gulp, as his gaze snagged on the neatly inscribed name: *"Alicia"*. The letters seemed to pulsate on the page, burning into his vision. *"Can it be? No...no...it's impossible,"* he gasped, the words catching like shards of glass in his windpipe. The small, airless office, heavy with the scent of old paper and dust, began to tilt and spin violently around him, the bookshelves blurring into a dizzying vortex, as his legs gave out, sending him crashing down onto his knees, the jarring impact barely registering. A flash of memory, sharp and unwelcome as a splinter, pierced through the swirling chaos. He remembered being there with her. The clinking of crystal and polite murmur of voices from the dinner party at his sister's house, the smell of roasted lamb and expensive perfume, flooded his senses. That night; the way she had smiled at him – a slow, unsettling curve of her lips that didn't quite reach her eyes. Something was profoundly off about it, the way her usually warm, dark brown eyes had sometimes looked utterly black, like bottomless wells of shadow. A cold dread snaked its way up his spine. A wave of nausea, sharp and metallic, surged in his stomach, clawing at his throat as he wondered, *"What is it? What *is* it, Rachel? What have you done?"* "ARE YOU HERE NOW?" he ripped the words from his burning lungs, his voice raw and ragged, bouncing off the silent walls. "CAN YOU HEAR ME NOW?! I CAN'T, Rachel...I *can't*!" His desperate shouting tore into choked, shuddering sobs, the sound tearing through the oppressive quiet of the room as he finally crumpled, a broken heap, onto the rough wooden floor, the dust motes dancing uncaringly in the lone shaft of moonlight.

A Bangkok Airways flight out of Chiang Mai Airport to Thailand's capital city was cancelled after a fire lantern was sucked into the plane's engine. A number of lanterns were spotted in and around the airport as the Airbus A320 taxied to its stand, with one coming too close for comfort and getting stuck to the turbine fan. The plane was scheduled to return to Bangkok's Suvarnabhumi airport after landing in Chiang Mai on New Year's Day.

The lantern was discovered in the fan turbine of the plane at Chiang Mai Airport, forcing the flight's cancellation. The aircraft was later flown back to Bangkok without passengers for further testing, leaving travelers stranded at Chiang Mai.

This incident is part of a growing pattern of dangerous encounters between traditional celebrations and modern aviation. Back in November during the Yi Peng festival, thousands of lanterns, known as khom loy, lit up the night sky like a swarm of jellyfish. More than 150 flights in and out of Chiang Mai were cancelled or delayed during the peak of the holiday, from November 5 to November 7, affecting around 20,000 passengers.

The festival coincides with Thailand's Loi Krathong festival, during which Thais float small rafts on rivers to ask for forgiveness from the goddess of water. They also believe that launching lanterns will rid them of bad fortune. Several airlines, including Thai Airways, were forced to alter their flight schedules to accommodate the lantern floating, which takes place mainly after dark.

In response to these mounting safety concerns, Thai authorities have implemented strict countermeasures. The air traffic control centre in Chiang Mai province declared a three-mile exclusion zone around the airport where people are prohibited from launching lanterns during festivals. Speaking to Straits Times, Royal Thai Police spokesman Pol Maj-General Prawut Thawornsiri said: "Police have set clear measures about releasing 'kom loy' (flying lanterns). It can be done after 9pm when air traffic is cleared up, but during the New Year holiday, many people released their lanterns in the evening."

The consequences for violating these safety measures are severe and uncompromising. "Releasing paper lanterns near airports can be very dangerous to the safety of aircraft and it is a criminal offence," said Police Major General Amnuay Nimmano. "Those who violate the law will be dealt with accordingly and could face life in prison or the death penalty."

The stark reality is clear: what begins as a beautiful cultural tradition seeking to wash away bad fortune can end in the ultimate penalty—a reminder that in the delicate balance between preserving heritage and ensuring public safety, the stakes couldn't be higher.

Though my upbringing was steeped in country music, it was largely the sonic landscape of my parents' era, my own musical inclinations having gravitated towards rock and roll. Yet, contemporary country music has since captured my profound admiration. Josh Turner, in particular, possesses a voice of remarkable richness and profound depth, interpreting both modern compositions and timeless classics with exceptional skill. My daughter, with characteristic youthful observation, posited that my acquisition of the album was influenced by his physical attractiveness. Unbeknownst to me at the time, his visual appeal played no part in my decision; my appreciation was singularly drawn to the exquisite timbre of his voice. Indeed, subsequent visual confirmation revealed an undeniably agreeable countenance. I deeply admire his decision to commence the album with a gospel composition, a track of infectious appeal and vocal accessibility. Conventionally, even devout artists might relegate such spiritual expressions to the album's conclusion, perhaps as a perfunctory gesture. Yet, he boldly positions his faith at the very forefront of his artistic offering. One anticipates with keen interest the future artistic endeavors of this remarkable artist.

Educational Ratings For Digital Content Launched
An educational ratings system for digital content, announced last May, has now launched. It includes ratings for over 150 mobile apps, games, and websites, with hundreds more expected soon, according to a press release from Common Sense Media.
The system was created by Common Sense Media, a San Francisco-based group that monitors media for young people, in partnership with the Chicago-based Susan Crown Foundation. It builds on Common Sense Media's existing way of reviewing popular movies and games to decide what's appropriate for different ages and how good they are.
Just as current reviews check movies and video games for violence, sexual content, and language, these new reviews will also look at how much math, science, and language arts content products contain. They will also see how well products help build skills like critical thinking, creativity, and teamwork. This effort is partly a response to research by Common Sense Media that found parents didn't trust claims that digital products were educational, according to the release.
The ratings are made using a combination of input from academic experts, teachers, parents, and current research on learning skills, according to the release. They will apply to digital media made for everyone, as well as to media created specifically for an educational audience.

Shadows crept along the walls as my parents gradually powered down the generator. “But WHY didn’t we listen?” I pressed, staring at my mom. She turned to me, wearing that same resigned smile she’d carried for months. Once again, she recounted our story, her words tinged with the steadfast affection and caution of a mother desperate to shield her child from the horrors—war, ruin, and death—ravaging everyone outside our carefully locked haven. “CLOSING DOORS IN 20,” blared a cold, mechanical voice from the speakers, echoing off the concrete. “It’s bedtime now, sweetheart. Tomorrow means everything,” she murmured, falling back into my dad’s arms as the last candle’s glow spilled across their faces—her gaze fixed on me as if I were her whole world. The doors groaned and slid shut with a finality that rattled my chest. “But, Mom, I don’t want to be saved.”

"Let me make this unequivocally clear: cease throwing your clothes at me! It's not merely unsanitary and disgusting; it's entirely unbefitting given I have absolutely no recognition of your person!" "I love you!" "Desist immediately! Withdraw from my presence!" "..but" "OUT!" And with that, I slammed the door. Idiots. While initially amusing, this persistent intrusion quickly transcended mere annoyance after the first month. The supposed sounds of love now elicit not happiness, but a profound and utterly disruptive irritation. Returning to my computer room, I began to browse through the daily reports, and contacted my research executive. "Marely?" "Yes, sir?" "I require an immediate and definitive anecdote to this pervasive love potion. Its continued presence has become an intolerable nuisance, demanding a conclusive resolution." And with that, I flew off on a solid gold private jet into the sunset. Being a trillionaire, one finds such conveniences utterly essential.

Ahead of UKIP’s spring conference, Nigel Farage said the party would back future Tory budgets if they helped eliminate the deficit by 2018. He criticized George Osborne for missing deficit targets since 2010, blaming a failure to make “tough choices.” Farage outlined UKIP’s plans to leave the EU, cancel HS2, and cut foreign aid to save money.

Speaking in Margate, Farage expressed optimism about UKIP’s general election prospects, predicting more UKIP MPs and positioning the party as Labour’s main opposition in northern England. He called for a positive, hopeful campaign despite a hostile political establishment, urging candidates to “turn the other cheek” and focus on UKIP’s message.

With two MPs already, UKIP aimed to increase its numbers and possibly hold the balance of power in a hung Parliament. While Conservatives dismissed post-election deals, Farage—standing in Thanet South—said he’d do “a deal with the devil” for an early EU referendum and would back Tory budgets only if they stuck to promised deficit reductions.

Farage criticized the Conservative-Liberal Democrat coalition for failing to eliminate the deficit, arguing that leaving the EU would save £8bn annually, foreign aid cuts would free £9bn, and scrapping HS2 could save £50bn.

Deputy leader Paul Nuttall highlighted the battle over the UK’s future post-Scottish referendum and criticized the SNP’s stance on English legislation. Immigration spokesman Stephen Woolfe blamed migration for strained schools and health services.

The Conservatives say they aim for an absolute surplus by 2019; Labour promises a day-to-day surplus by 2020.

Young, I made a pact with my best friend Neil: if unwed by twenty-one, we'd marry. Neil is still my best friend. I was his best(wo) man six years ago. He has two wonderful children that call me Auntie. I dislike his wife. Yesterday I came home late, busy with a new coding script for my company's website project. Neil lay on my couch, covered in tears. My heart in my stomach from his pain, I wrapped my arms around him, waiting for him to speak. Sobbing into my shoulder, he spoke with a broken, unrecognizable voice: "I don't know what I did wrong. How could she? And she...blamed it...I can't do this anymore. She took..." "Take your time," I murmured, gently stroking his hair. Taking a deep breath, Neil cried out, "Leigha cheated on me." The fire within me was scalding. A heat I'd never known rose from my heart, reaching to burn and slash his temptress wife. I restrained my anger to comfort my broken friend. His desolate eyes broke my spirit more than any physical pain would. "What did I do wrong?" he asked in shattered submission. I had no answer. We lay on the couch, pressed together in an intimate embrace. He clutched my damp shirt, as if I were his bungee cord off a bridge. My arms around him, he settled into my embrace. His harsh, broken breaths evened out as he cried himself into unconsciousness. I sighed, staring at his sleeping face. I stroked his cheek with my thumb, and smiled as he nuzzled into my touch. I closed my eyes, praying he'd find happiness. Morning, my phone had nine text messages and three voicemails, all from his wife. Glancing at his sleeping face, my heart flipped. I powered off my phone, throwing it across the room. I pressed back into his embrace.

The killing of Harambe, a male gorilla at the Cincinnati Zoo, sparked widespread controversy and ignited debates on animal rights, parental responsibility, and zoo policies. Many argued that Harambe did not intend to harm the four-year-old boy who fell into his enclosure, citing the viral hashtag #JusticeForHarambe. Supporters voiced their opinions across social media, with Twitter user StrayanRepublic noting, "#HARAMBE wasn't dragging him to kill him... he was protecting the child from the threat of screaming tourists," while user Kenz lamented, "It's so sad that an endangered animal had to be put down because of careless parenting." These perspectives reflect the profound sense of loss felt by individuals who regarded Harambe as an innocent victim.

Others, however, placed blame on the child's parents, claiming negligence led to the incident. Over 60,000 people signed a petition demanding the parents be held accountable for the events leading to Harambe's death. The controversy intensified as comparisons were drawn to similar incidents worldwide, including the shooting of two lions in a Chilean zoo after a man entered their pen in an apparent suicide attempt. This led to further questions about zoo preparedness, with social media user Andrue asking, "Why don't zoos have instant acting tranquilizer?"

The zoo’s decision to kill Harambe followed a tense situation in which the gorilla grabbed and dragged the child after the boy had climbed over a barrier and fallen nearly ten feet into the moat. Video footage shows Harambe moving the child through the shallow water, leading to fears for the boy's safety. According to zoo director Thane Maynard, officials made the difficult but necessary decision to shoot Harambe, emphasizing, “They made the right choice because they saved that little boy’s life. It could have been very bad.” Maynard further explained that a tranquilizer would not have worked quickly enough and admitted, “Although the boy was not under attack, he certainly was at risk.”

Harambe’s death is recognized as a significant loss for both the Cincinnati Zoo and the global population of critically endangered western lowland gorillas. Born in captivity in Texas and transferred to Cincinnati in 2014, Harambe was expected to contribute to the zoo’s successful gorilla breeding program. The western lowland gorilla, native to West African rainforests, remains the most numerous gorilla subspecies, yet exact population numbers remain unclear due to their endangered status.

This incident also recalls the 1986 case in Jersey, UK, when a silverback named Jambo famously protected an unconscious boy who had fallen into his enclosure, an event celebrated as a moment of animal empathy. However, experts caution against drawing direct parallels, as every incident involves unique circumstances.

In the aftermath of Harambe's death, the Cincinnati Zoo temporarily closed its gorilla exhibit and publicly acknowledged the gravity of the loss. “We are all devastated that this tragic accident resulted in the death of a critically endangered gorilla. This is a huge loss for the zoo family and the gorilla population worldwide,” Maynard stated.

Ultimately, the Harambe incident highlights the complex ethical questions that arise when human and animal lives intersect under extraordinary circumstances. It has prompted calls for stricter safety protocols at zoos, greater public understanding of animal behavior, and an ongoing dialogue about the responsibilities of both institutions and individuals in preventing future tragedies.

For students to truly understand their world, they often need to use tools to gather information, transforming them into curious explorers discovering hidden wonders. Some tools will help them see fascinating parts of their surrounding world that they would otherwise miss completely—tiny details that spark amazement and wonder. These magical tools include magnifying glasses and microscopes that reveal intricate patterns, textures, and structures invisible to the naked eye. The lesson can be conducted as an engaging learning center where students work collaboratively in small groups, or as an interactive teacher-led whole class instruction filled with "aha!" moments and excited discoveries. These activities can be used along with other captivating hands-on experiments discussed in the inspiring book Microscopes and Magnifying Lenses by Janice VanCleave.

He stood in shock as the world disappeared. He didn't know what to do, what could he possibly think? A small part of him seemed to hear a voice, or was someone really talking to him? A face looked down into his with what he could only assume was concern. Down? He was sitting. When did I...The face felt familiar, he thought, as though he might know it if he could just grasp the elusive memory. The voice seemed to belong to the face, he vaguely realized. The voice had told him what happened, had destroyed him with what it claimed was its message. 'She's gone,' it had said. Not it, he. Was it really a friend, telling him how his wife had supposedly been found, a strong, thin cord holding her to the ceiling fan? Could that be true?

The memory tried to come back, threatening to remove the world again, but this time he held on, if only just. What was he to do? He felt frozen. Without her, he couldn't possibly know what to do with himself. His whole world had been built with her at the center, and now...Why? Why would she leave? Why would she ever take such a drastic path? It didn't make sense.

She had always been the smart one, he recalled, always knowing what to do. Had it been his fault? Had he driven her to this? Could he possibly have done something so terrible? The thought made his head spin, and his stomach with it. She had been the smart one... She had been the smart one. She wouldn't do that, would she? She wouldn't just leave. She was too smart for that. Wasn't she?

Suddenly, he thought he heard her voice. Was she talking to him? He glanced up from the carpet pattern. She was there, right there, standing in front of him, smiling softly. He couldn't make out what she was saying, but it didn't matter. She was there! Or was she? He got up and walked to her. It took longer than it should have; the air felt solid, compressing him like heavy water. His so-called friend looked at him with concern, asking him something about his feelings, feigning not to notice his beautiful wife a few feet away. Was he truly blind, or just playing a cruel game?

It was a trick. It must be a trick. The man, the stranger, wanted him to believe his wife was gone. Why? It was a trick. She was leading him to something. What was it? The stranger put a hand on his shoulder, trying to stop him, to convince him of the lie. He kept going, step by step, to the counter. Was he truly going to fall for it?

It was an old bookmark, a long, thin piece of brass, with an intricate orchid carved into the top. She had had the bookmark since childhood, and he thought he remembered countless paperbacks lying around the house with that flower blooming gracefully above the pages. Hardly a day had ever gone by when she had not been halfway into a book. Or had it? She had been discerning, too. Only the good stuff. She wouldn't ever settle for a less-than-brilliant author. Could that be right? The hand on his shoulder was shaking him, and the voice sounded frantic in the distance. For a moment, there was a small part of him that faintly wondered what the voice was going on about, what could be so important? And then it died.
-------------------
Smiling, he opened the front door. The sun was shining. He and his wife stepped outside and began walking to the nearest library, so that she could read and read to her heart's content. They walked away, leaving behind a small brass orchid blooming gracefully from a stranger's throat. He didn't need the keepsake, after all. He had his beautiful wife. Forever.

**Historical Context**
The book has a great deal of mostly accurate historical information, especially for those who practice that particular kung fu style.

**Conceptual Depth and Audience**
Some of the concepts Mr Co discusses in his book, although put in a simple form for understanding, could be appreciated only by those who are fairly well versed in the ngo cho kun kung fu system.

**Role of an Instructor**
Simply reading the book and learning the two forms in it do not replace an instructor who knows the many facets of the system. However, the book does provide a great introduction to some of the concepts.

Tiny diamonds of sand slip quietly beneath her bare feet as she walks the well-worn path to the beach. The slope is far too steep to ride down, though that never stopped the boys, especially Bobby, from trying. That was earlier in the summer, when the evenings still held sunlight, and they hadn’t attempted it again after the night Kyle left in an ambulance. She’s too cautious to risk such a reckless descent—unlike the boys—so she wheels her old bike, now two sizes too small, relishing the feel of sand massaging her soles. She can hear the others on the shore already: Bobby’s voice booming above the rest, Kyle grumbling about sand in his cast, Silvia shrieking as someone splashes her with sun-warmed seawater. Silvia is always flirting. 

For the first summer she can remember, the beach has changed. It used to feel like a favorite grandparent, welcoming her with warmth and little gifts. Now it feels like a stranger at the door—someone familiar, but forever altered, distant and secretive. She blames Silvia, the city girl whose family bought the big cottage Mr. Gregory once filled with paint and canvases. Her parents tell her Mr. Gregory doesn’t paint anymore; he moved to the city for work. The beach feels emptier without the wild-haired artist shouting at them for interrupting him. She had loved watching him work. 

This was supposed to be her and Bobby’s summer. Long, grey afternoons at boarding school in the mountains had been bearable only because she could picture Bobby’s floppy blonde hair and tanned hand flicking it from his blue eyes. Of course Silvia had to kiss him. She doesn’t know exactly what Silvia is, but she’s certain it’s not something decent. 

Father says they won’t return next year. She imagines Silvia’s family buying their cottage too, taking over the swing on the porch with Bobby—an idea that burns. Down on the sand, Silvia’s cries echo, calling to Bobby to join her, teasing Kyle for being afraid. The girl stops, listening to the changed beach, feeling the unfamiliar sand, tasting salt in the air as if for the first time. Then she turns and walks back up the hill, leaving the beach behind on the final night of summer.

Previous Challenge Entry (Level 3 - Advanced)
Topic: Patience (08/21/08)
**TITLE: Prudance and Patience**
LEAVE COMMENT ON ARTICLE
SEND A PRIVATE COMMENT
ADD TO MY FAVORITES
As a child she always made sure to wait on her friends, family and others and whenever she felt “put upon” by anyone, she was reminded by her mother that, **“Prudence, all good things in life come only when you’re patient and kind with others!”** And, so she listened attentively and kept those words alive.
That is, until one November day when as a young woman, she found herself waiting on a friend of hers, a young woman by the name of Katrina. **Katrina was just the opposite of Prudence. She was an impetuous woman, who wanted the very best out of life and she wanted it, now!**
Well, that November day Prudence was waiting on Katrina to make up her mind about going back to college with her. They were to be roommates at Ohio Central College and a decision had to be made, soon.
**Prudence had been waiting over a month** and by her standards was getting a bit “ticked off” by Katrina’s off-handed way of waiting till the last minute.
Well, in her mind, **time was up! Today was D-Day! She needed to know and she would find out, no matter the cost!**
The two always met on Saturday for shopping and running errands, so this would be prime time to talk with her about the matter.
They met at the shopping mall as usual around 10 and began their shopping. Around 12 they stopped for lunch and that is when Prudence brought up the matter.
After enjoying a nice salad she said, **“Katrina, we need to get going on our plans for college. If we wait much longer, we’re not going to be able to get in. I need to know if you still want to go and if we can be roommates.”**
Katrina was fiddling with her lemonade, dropping in more sugar and looked around the mall, as if trying to avoid the question. Clearly, she felt uncomfortable with the question.
“Prude…you know how much I want to go but I’m not sure if I can…” Her eyes began to wander all over the cafeteria and she looked like she wanted to run…
“Kat, something is bothering you. Please tell me. We’ve been friends for a long time and I know that you want to go. Is someone or some “thing” preventing you from making a decision?”
Katrina looked sad, with her head down. So sad in fact that Prudence thought that her friend was ill.
Katrina continued, “Prude, I didn’t want to say anything because I wasn’t sure how you would take it but I guess it’s time to ‘fess up.’”
She continued, avoiding all the time looking her friend in the eye. **“Prude, I just don’t think I can handle the pressure—the books, instructors and taking notes! I just don’t think I have what it takes to do it…”**
As her friend talked, she began to form some ideas in her mind on how to respond.
**“Kat, I hear what you’re saying and I want you to know that I have those same doubts, too! But, you know what? We can help each other and especially since we’re taking the same courses in Psychology, we can make sure that we’re both on track and we can also work on assignments together. It won’t be easy but with hard work and God’s help, we’ll make it!”**
Katrina listened and as she heard her friend’s words, she began thinking that yes, maybe it just might work.
They continued on with the conversation, coming to the conclusion that they would give it their best efforts. **So often, as with this case, patience is so worth it!**
